source_quote: Figure 1 Patient disposition: Death 0-12 weeks: Calcium Acetate n=1, Placebo n=1. 46 allocated to Calcium Acetate, 64 allocated to Placebo.
the proportions of those with hypercalcemia were 13.5% and 0%, respectively. At 12 weeks, calcium acetate N=37, placebo N=41 completers.

Nausea not individually reported in main paper. Table 3 reports Gastrointestinal disorders as related AE: calcium acetate 3 (6.5%) vs placebo 6 (9.4%). Detailed AE breakdown in Additional file 1: B (supplement, not in main PDF).

in Group 1 from 8.31 +/- 2.67 mg/dL to 5.11 +/- 1.49 mg/dL; in Group 2 from 8.79 +/- 2.11 mg/dL to 4.02 +/- 1.30 mg/dL. Stage III-IV patients: Group 1 n=67, Group 2 n=64. Note: abstract reports values as mean +/- SEM (0.18 and 0.12) but detailed text on p5-6 gives SD values (1.49 and 1.30).

Table 4: Serum calcium (mg/dL) after 6 months: Group 1 9.5 +/- 0.2, Group 2 10.7 +/- 0.1. Results expressed as mean +/- SEM. SD estimated as SEM*sqrt(n): Group 1 SD=0.2*sqrt(75)=1.73, Group 2 SD=0.1*sqrt(75)=0.87.
Table 5: PTH (pg/mL) after 6 months: Group 1 69 +/- 3, Group 2 68 +/- 5. Results expressed as mean +/- SEM. SD estimated as SEM*sqrt(n): Group 1 SD=3*sqrt(75)=25.98, Group 2 SD=5*sqrt(75)=43.30.

Death counts not reported in text. Patient disposition (Figure 2) is an image not extractable by text parser. Table 4 reports serious TEAEs: colestilan 31/76 (40.8%), sevelamer 48/124 (38.7%) but does not break out deaths specifically.
